divergence_notes: jayyhk erdos403_complete/erdos_403 state a complete classification (IsErdos403Solution m s iff the explicit five-solution disjunction); this draft states only the finiteness asked verbatim by the problem text ('only finitely many solutions'), as answer(True) <-> Set.Finite.

jayyhk encodes a solution via the named predicate IsErdos403Solution (per its docstring: 1 <= a_1 < a_2 < ... < a_k over a Finset); this draft inlines the condition on a pair (m, s) : N x Finset N as (forall a in s, 0 < a) and 2^m = sum over a in s of a!, with distinctness given by Finset membership.
jayyhk erdos403 quantifies over m alone (existence of a solution set, m in {0,1,3,5,7}); this draft's solution set counts (m, s) pairs, so finiteness is over full solutions, not just exponents.
This draft omits jayyhk's explicit solution list entirely (no enumeration of {1}, {2}, {2,3}, {2,3,4}, {2,3,5}); the enumeration is the proof-side content, not the question.
